text.title: Testimonial Harvest SOP Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Testimonial Harvest SOP application MUST enforce, plus a skip-this-methodology fallback. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Harvest MUST trigger at project handover (final deliverable accepted), not at random later dates.
rule.rationale: Peak satisfaction sits at acceptance; delays collapse response rate and quote quality.

rule.statement: Script MUST use the 3-question form: before-state / outcome / what-changed. Open-ended asks are rejected.
rule.rationale: The 3-question script extracts result-specific quotes; open-ended asks yield generic praise.

rule.statement: Output MUST be both a written quote AND a Loom video; written-only or video-only is rejected.
rule.rationale: Written quotes serve landing pages; video serves case studies; both are non-substitutable surfaces.

rule.statement: Each harvest MUST capture ≥1 outcome metric (number, percentage, or named milestone).
rule.rationale: Metric-free quotes do not convert; the metric is the social-proof payload.

rule.statement: Harvest MUST include explicit publication consent + usage rights scope (web / ads / collateral). Implicit consent is rejected.
rule.rationale: Reusing testimonials beyond original consent creates legal exposure and breaks customer trust.

rule.statement: If any Applies-If precondition fails OR any Skip-If condition triggers, do NOT apply this methodology. Emit a skip-record naming the failed precondition / triggered skip, and route the task to the correct methodology (or none).
rule.rationale: Methodology hygiene: mis-application produces worse artefacts than no methodology because downstream consumers trust the schema; the skip-record makes non-application traceable.
